session: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (2nd session of the day — the one that superclauded up after the roster+launcher build)
author: CC
in_flight: RESTART PROVEN + first code-seam landed. On boot this session confirmed the 7 skills load as slash-commands (measured: /loadslate, /intent, /sorted all fired live) — closing the restart-proof threads from the prior slate (2026-07-15-build-roster-and-launcher). Then ran the full work loop on a real node for the first time: /intent -> /sorted cast `default-superclaude-to-200k`, the first `code-seam` node. Built + interim-proved it: launchers/superclaude FILE_DEFAULT_200K flipped 0->1, so a bare `superclaude` now resolves to 200K; --1m still opts up (command line wins); all 6 --dry-run proof cases green. The why was enriched in BOTH the script comment and launchers/intention+why.json (stewardship: Claude can't self-/compact, so 1M balloons past Akien's notification threshold — 200K makes it physics not discipline; traces Telos 6 + Law 4). PROOF.md updated in lockstep (the completeness gate caught that the proof hard-coded the old default). New governing structure this session: the `code-seam` node-class was written + ratified by Akien (signature gate), with a durability gate folded in on his refinement — a code-seam is not done until tested AND committed.
next_direction: After this exit+restart, in order: (1) LIVE PROOF — confirm a bare `superclaude` (no flags) comes up in 200K; that is the still-open falsifier on the default-superclaude-to-200k ticket and closes it. (2) Formalize the founding intention 'get Akien OUT of running the system' as a real /intent node — it surfaced this session as the why behind commit-often/don't-ask and deserves its own trace to Telos. (3) Build cairnmap — the derivation gate that retires all 7 skills' proof IOUs at once. Then continue the spine (CP/diagnostic base -> tester + kernel-owned network -> db ...).
open_threads: LIVE PROOF (Akien, on restart): does a bare `superclaude` come up 200K? All --dry-run cases pass; the live launch is the real measurement (Law 3). If it comes up 1M, the file-default branch isn't winning — check env inheritance (a set CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT would report '200K (environment)' not 'file default'; unset it to test the file default).

STANDING GUIDANCE banked this session (Akien): commit OFTEN and autonomously — never ask 'should I commit?'; commit messages are notes to future selves; push only at 'smells like done'. Rationale = the get-Akien-out-of-the-loop intention. Memory: commit-often-dont-ask.

code-seam prove_gate is the tester's proof gate — an IOU (tester not built). Until then a code-seam is proved hand-run via its proofs/PROOF.md. Same shape as the skills' derivation-gate IOU to cairnmap.

GitHub remotes still unconfigured for both repos — this exit's commits are LOCAL-ONLY (no push). 'Push at smells-like-done' can't fire until remotes exist; configuring them is a candidate near-term node.
Q6 (intention envelope schema) still open — every new record (code-seam.json, the ticket, this slate) carries schema_version 'v0 ... migrates when Q6 lands'.
node_classes/ now holds skill + concept-piece + code-seam. code doesn't exist for other classes yet (discovered as demanded).

Meta: this session is itself specimen #1 — the work loop (/intent -> /sorted -> cast -> build -> interim-prove) ran on a real node and held; Akien injected a mid-flow correction (commit-don't-ask) that got folded into structure (the code-seam durability gate) rather than just remembered.
